description:
You are assisting a performance engineer who just finished profiling an application.  
    The profiler generated one summary file in UTF-16 little-endian format with a BOM:

        /home/user/profiling/output_utf16.txt

    The engineer’s tools cannot ingest UTF-16, so the file must be converted to UTF-8.  
    Your objectives:

    1. Convert /home/user/profiling/output_utf16.txt to UTF-8 and write the result to

           /home/user/profiling/output_utf8.txt

       • The textual content must remain byte-for-byte identical when viewed as Unicode characters; only the encoding changes.  
       • The UTF-8 file must NOT contain a UTF-8 BOM.  
       • Line endings must stay as Unix LF.

    2. Generate a tiny conversion log so that automated tests can verify success.  
       Create a new file

           /home/user/profiling/conversion.log

       The file must contain exactly two lines (including the trailing newline on each line!):

           Conversion Successful
           output_utf8.txt created

       No extra spaces, BOMs, blank lines or carriage returns are allowed.

    Verification notes for yourself (do NOT print these): the grader will
    1) verify that /home/user/profiling/output_utf8.txt exists,
    2) confirm it is valid UTF-8,
    3) ensure its decoded text is identical to the original UTF-16 file, and
    4) check that /home/user/profiling/conversion.log contains the two exact lines shown above.

    Use only standard CLI tooling available on a typical Linux distribution.
truth:

    <!-- Expected files after the agent finishes -->

            Profile Summary\n
            Function compute() took 12ms\n
            Function save_results() took 8ms\n
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/profiling/conversion.log" encoding="UTF-8" bom="no">
            Conversion Successful\n
            output_utf8.txt created\n
        </file>
    </expected_files>
